theme: harbor master mooring boats along one long quay
title: Breakwater Berths: Sculpting the Empty Slips of a Ring Marina
strategies: home-order first-fit: moor each boat at the earliest free berth in its reach window (checker baseline)
frequency-first first-fit: give high-traffic boats priority for their home berth to cut successful-lookup cost, ignoring where empties land
breakwater sculpting: treat empty slips as a positionable resource, relocating them (by shifting whole blocks of boats one berth forward) into high-absent-weight zones so miss-scans terminate quickly
per-run trade-off: pay a little displacement to open an empty just downstream of a hot zone, cutting the forward miss-distance of every berth in it
